message:
fix: inject original task into implementer context at depth 0

When the coordinator spawns an implementer, automatically prepend the
original user task in an <original-task> block. This structurally prevents
delegation information loss — the implementer always sees the full spec
regardless of how the coordinator paraphrases in its delegation.

Root cause: the coordinator consistently paraphrases task descriptions
even when explicitly told to forward them verbatim. Format specs, exact
content strings, and schema details get lost. This was confirmed across
3 tasks (nginx-request-logging, multi-source-data-merger,
log-summary-date-ranges) and persisted even after prompt-level fixes.

Only applies at depth 0 (coordinator → implementer), not deeper spawns
where the task is already coordinator-authored.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
